value:
Data from Layoffs.fyi showed that more than 101,550 employees across 120 technology companies have been laid off so far this year.
ADVERTISEMENT
Cybersecurity and internet infrastructure company Cloudflare is set to reduce around 20% of its global workforce, impacting more than 1,100 employees, as the company restructures operations around increased adoption of artificial intelligence tools.
The company had 5,156 full-time employees at the end of 2025.
The layoffs were announced in an internal note by co-founders Matthew Prince and Michelle Zatlyn, who said the company was redesigning its teams and workflows for what it described as the “agentic AI era”.
According to the note, Cloudflare’s internal use of AI tools has increased more than sixfold over the past three months, significantly changing the way employees operate across functions.
Also read: Bumble to scrap swiping feature as CEO signals major app overhaul amid user decline
“Today’s actions are not a cost-cutting exercise or an assessment of individuals’ performance,” the founders said in the communication to employees. “They are about Cloudflare defining how a world-class, high-growth company operates and creates value in the agentic AI era.”
Prince also told The Wall Street Journal that the company’s AI-first operating model is intended to make Cloudflare “faster and more innovative”.
Cloudflare expects to incur charges between $140 million and $150 million related to the layoffs during the second quarter.
The company joins a growing list of technology firms announcing workforce reductions tied to AI-led operational restructuring. This week, crypto exchange Coinbase and enterprise software company Freshworks also announced job cuts linked to AI-driven changes in operations. Freshworks said it would reduce 11% of its global workforce, affecting nearly 500 employees, while Coinbase plans to cut 14% of staff.
Also read: Anthropic unveils ‘Dreams’ feature for Claude to enable self-improving AI agents
Data from Layoffs.fyi showed that more than 101,550 employees across 120 technology companies have been laid off so far this year.
Alongside the restructuring announcement, Cloudflare reported first-quarter financial results. The company posted a net loss of $22.9 million for the quarter, compared with a loss of $38.5 million during the same period last year.
Revenue rose to $639.8 million from $479.1 million a year earlier, exceeding analyst expectations of $620.8 million.
For the second quarter, Cloudflare projected revenue between $664 million and $665 million, slightly below analyst estimates of $665.3 million, while adjusted earnings guidance stood at 27 cents per share.
Despite reporting better-than-expected quarterly revenue, Cloudflare shares fell around 19% in extended trading after the announcement. The stock had closed 3.3% higher at $256.79 on the Nasdaq earlier in the day. (Inputs from ET report)
